People need to read more about Delia Owens and her husband.  In 1996, they were the subject of an ABC News expose that linked them to the televised murder of an African man.  They are still wanted in Zambia in connection with the murder.  She has based this story loosely on that experience.  She also racistly names animals in the book after her black servants in Africa and makes them sound like simpletons.  Allegations of murder and racism have dogged Delia Owens for 30 years.  Rich suburban white women worship her because they don't know who she really is.

I see a lot of comments claiming that Kaya looks too "normal" in the trailer and they expected her to look more wild. I will say that you can see from (spoiler) how she spends her money later on in the book that she has an idea of what normal life is, and she's seen people and what they look like, not to mention she does live in a house, just not a "regular" or updated one. Just because she spends most of her time out in the wild doesnt mean she rolls around in the mud all day. Not to mention, she probably has an internal desire to emulate her mother in some way. Not criticizing anyone's opinions to be clear, but I saw this comment quite a bit and wanted to share my thoughts. In a way, expecting her to look like a creature is almost like how the people in town thought of her, and most of them who hadnt seen her were surprised she was still just a girl
